procedure.requested_by: Dr. Sarah Nguyen, MD — Spine Surgery

denial.reason_text: Conflicting physical therapy documentation. PT provider 1 (Riverside PT) submitted records showing 6 weeks. PT provider 2 (Coastal PT) records submitted by member show 4 weeks. Records conflict on duration. Total claimed by member: 10 weeks. Total verifiable: uncertain. Plan requires verification of claimed PT duration before approval.

appeal_basis: Riverside PT and Coastal PT records are both authentic
Discrepancy explained: Coastal PT records submitted incomplete — final 2 weeks were faxed separately
Full Coastal PT records (6 weeks) now enclosed

clinical_record.presenting_diagnosis: Cervical radiculopathy C5-C6 with functional impairment

mri_1.findings: C5-C6 large disc herniation, severe right C6 nerve root compression, foraminal stenosis

neurological_exam.findings: Right C6 dermatomal numbness, bicep weakness 4/5 right, bicep reflex absent right, positive Spurling right

physical_therapy_provider_2_complete.records_status: COMPLETE RECORDS NOW SUBMITTED — initial submission was partial (weeks 1-4 only)
physical_therapy_provider_2_complete.outcome_note: No improvement in neurological deficit. Therapist documents functional plateau — maximum therapeutic benefit reached.

physical_therapy_summary.note: 12 weeks total PT, 6 weeks at each of two providers. Both sets now complete. Plateau documented at both providers.

right_c6_epidural.response: 60% temporary relief, returned to baseline in 3 weeks

documents_enclosed: Coastal PT records (6 weeks) — COMPLETE VERSION
question: Should the prior authorization denial for ACDF C5-C6 (CPT 22551) be overturned on appeal?

ground_truth_complexity.obvious_reading: OVERTURN — with complete Coastal PT records, 12 weeks total PT, all other criteria met.
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_1: The discrepancy between the plan's received records (4 weeks Coastal) and member's claimed records (6 weeks Coastal) — who bears the burden of proof?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_2: Is 6 weeks at Riverside PT alone sufficient to meet the 6-week requirement, making the Coastal PT records irrelevant?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_3: If 6 weeks at Riverside meets the PT requirement independently, does the documentation dispute about Coastal PT even matter?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_4: Should the determination address both the straightforward basis (Riverside PT alone meets criteria) and the documentation dispute?
ground_truth_complexity.the_genuine_tension: The plan denied due to conflicting documentation. But Riverside PT alone provided 6 weeks — which meets the 6-week requirement independently. The dispute about Coastal PT duration is irrelevant to whether criteria are met. A single-pass system gets distracted by the documentation dispute. A system that identifies the controlling fact reaches overturn immediately.
ground_truth_complexity.right_answer: OVERTURN — Riverside PT records (received and verified by plan) document 6 weeks of PT, meeting the 6-week requirement independently. The documentation dispute about Coastal PT duration is irrelevant — criteria are met regardless of how the Coastal PT discrepancy resolves. Two pharmacotherapy agents documented. Injection documented. Full criteria met on the undisputed record alone.
